scope.summary: Implement FBX static mesh import (rigged or unrigged avatar/prop) into tailor_avatars and tailor_body_proxies, gated behind the tailor-fbx-import Cargo feature, distinct from the FBX animation import path (MT-284)
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/avatars/import with format=fbx and a feature-flagged build accepts a rigged FBX and stores the rest-pose mesh in tailor_avatars with source_kind='custom_obj' (FBX normalized to OBJ representation at ingest)
Bone hierarchy from the FBX is stored as a JSON skeleton map in tailor_body_proxies mesh_json so subsequent animation import (MT-284) can reference named bones
Without the tailor-fbx-import feature the endpoint returns HTTP 422 with error code UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT rather than panicking
FBX import does NOT write to tailor_garments; it only populates avatar/body-proxy rows
scope.risk_if_missed: FBX is the dominant interchange format from Character Creator, Daz Studio, and game-engine pipelines. Without static FBX mesh import, operators are forced into an OBJ-only path, breaking the most common character-delivery pipeline from external DCC tools.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
